meta.name: Aloo Gobi
meta.subtitle: Potato & cauliflower sabzi with turmeric, cumin, and a tomato-ginger masala

source.name: SSA canonical cuisines/indian/recipes/aloo_gobi.json
notes: Deterministic weeknight-friendly aloo gobi using stovetop pan-roast + steam finish.
Optimized for consistent texture: browned potatoes, tender cauliflower, not mushy.

summary: A classic Indian dry curry: golden potatoes and tender cauliflower tossed in a fragrant turmeric-cumin masala with ginger, optional tomato, and a bright lemon finish.

yield.notes: Serves 4 as a side; 2–3 as a main with roti/rice and a protein.

notes: Naturally gluten-free and dairy-free as written.

optional.label: Nonstick skillet
optional.notes: Helps prevent potatoes from sticking during the browning stage.

ingredients.label: Potatoes, diced 3/4-inch
notes: Yukon gold preferred for creamy interior; russet also works (crisper edges).

ingredients.label: Cauliflower florets
notes: Cut into bite-size florets (about 5–6 cups). Keep pieces similar for even cooking.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil
notes: Can replace 1 tbsp with ghee for richer flavor (not vegan).

ingredients.label: Kashmiri chili powder (or mild chili powder)
notes: Add more for heat; Kashmiri gives color with mild warmth.

ingredients.label: Diced tomatoes
notes: Optional but recommended: helps create a light masala coating and balances starch.

ingredients.label: Water
notes: Used to steam-finish the potatoes/cauliflower.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: Start with 1 tsp early; finish with the rest to taste.

steps.title: Prep vegetables
instructions: Dice potatoes into 3/4-inch pieces and keep in a bowl of water to prevent browning; drain and pat dry before cooking.
Slice onion; mince garlic; grate ginger.
checks: Keep cauliflower florets similar size for even tenderness.

steps.title: Brown the potatoes
instructions: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add drained, well-dried potatoes and spread into an even layer.
Cook 8–10 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden in spots.
checks: Don’t stir constantly; give potatoes contact time to brown.
If sticking, reduce heat slightly and add a small splash of oil.

steps.title: Cook onion and bloom spices
instructions: Push potatoes to the side of the skillet (or remove temporarily to a bowl).
Add remaining 1 tbsp oil. Add onions and cook 3–4 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and ginger; cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Sprinkle in cumin, coriander, turmeric, and Kashmiri chili (if using). Stir 15–20 seconds to bloom.
If using diced tomatoes, add them now and cook 2 minutes to soften.
checks: Spices should smell toasted, not burnt—lower heat if needed.
Tomatoes are optional; they help prevent dryness and add tang.

steps.title: Add cauliflower and steam until tender
instructions: Return potatoes to the center (if removed). Add cauliflower and toss everything to coat in the masala.
Add water and salt (start with 1 tsp). Stir, then cover with a lid.
Reduce heat to medium and steam 10–12 minutes, stirring once halfway, until potatoes are tender and cauliflower is cooked but not mushy.
checks: If pan dries out, add 1–2 tbsp water and re-cover.
For a drier sabzi, remove lid for the last 2–3 minutes to evaporate moisture.

steps.title: Finish and season
instructions: Turn heat to low. Taste and adjust salt.
Stir in garam masala (optional) for aroma.
checks: Acid at the end brightens the dish.
Garam masala is best added at the end to preserve fragrance.

notes: Serve with roti/naan or rice. Great alongside dal or a simple lentil soup.
Add a cooling side like cucumber salad or raita (if not vegan).
Reheat in a skillet with a splash of water; finish with fresh lemon.
Not ideal to freeze: potatoes can turn grainy and cauliflower can soften.
Cool quickly before refrigerating.
Reheat to steaming hot throughout.
Parboil potatoes 6 minutes, drain, then pan-fry to crisp edges faster.
After steaming, uncover and cook 3–5 minutes to dry and lightly toast the masala onto the vegetables.
Add 1 cup frozen peas during the last 3 minutes of steaming.
Increase salt slightly and finish with extra lemon.
At the very beginning, heat oil and sizzle 1 tsp cumin seeds and 1/2 tsp mustard seeds until fragrant, then proceed with onions.
Optional: add 6–8 curry leaves for a South-Indian-leaning aroma.
Weeknight-friendly if paired with quick dal, eggs, or leftover protein.
Scales well for batch meal prep (best refrigerated, not frozen).
